title: platycerium alcicorne var. hillii
intro: Platycerium alcicorne var. hillii, also known as the Hill's staghorn, is a variety of staghorn fern native to the tropical forests of Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Platycerium alcicorne var. hillii include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve to treat various health issues.
